candidates.title: MESSENGER MDIS BDR Data Archive
candidates.reasoning: PDS4 bundle for MESSENGER MDIS map-projected basemap (BDR RDR) products that cover Mercury globally, including the north polar region where low-reflectance deposits occur. Stable bundle landing location is provided via the archive S3 path.

candidates.title: MESSENGER MDIS MAP PROJECTED BASEMAP ARCHIVE
candidates.reasoning: Legacy PDS3 volume corresponding to MDIS map-projected basemap products (BDR RDR). Useful for examining albedo/reflectance patterns at the north pole; included as the PDS3 counterpart to the PDS4 BDR bundle.

candidates.title: MESS MDIS MAP PROJECTED REGIONAL TARGETED MOSAIC ARCHIVE
candidates.reasoning: Regional targeted map-projected mosaics from MDIS, which can provide higher-detail context in specific north polar areas compared to global basemaps.

candidates.title: MESSENGER MDIS CALIBRATED DATA ARCHIVE
candidates.reasoning: Calibrated MDIS images (CDR) used to analyze reflectance/photometry at high latitudes; provides underlying image data for interpreting the nature of low-reflectance deposits.

candidates.title: MESSENGER MDIS UNCALIBRATED DATA ARCHIVE
candidates.reasoning: Raw MDIS image products (EDR) that can be reprocessed or compared with calibrated products when investigating low-reflectance deposits in the north polar region.

candidates.title: MESSENGER MDIS DEM Data Archive
candidates.reasoning: PDS4 bundle containing MDIS-derived digital elevation model products; complements imagery by providing topographic context for north polar low-reflectance deposits (e.g., relation to craters/terrain).

candidates.title: MESSENGER MDIS DIGITAL ELEVATION MODEL ARCHIVE
candidates.reasoning: Legacy PDS3 DEM volume for MDIS topography; included as the PDS3 counterpart to the PDS4 MDIS DEM bundle.

candidates.title: MESSENGER MDIS MAP PROJECTED MULTISPECTRAL ARCHIVE
candidates.reasoning: Map-projected multispectral products (MDR) that support compositional/colour context in addition to reflectance, useful when evaluating low-reflectance deposits at the north pole.

candidates.title: MESSENGER MDIS 3-COLOR MAP PROJECTED MULTISPECTRAL ARCHIVE
candidates.reasoning: 3-color map-projected MDIS products that can be used alongside basemaps to visually delineate low-reflectance material at high northern latitudes.

candidates.title: MESSENGER MDIS DERIVED DATA ARCHIVE
candidates.reasoning: MDIS derived geometry/ancillary products (DDR) that support photometric/geometry-aware analysis of reflectance features, relevant when assessing low-reflectance deposits from images.

3. Search Plan —
- Catalog-first discovery in the PDS dataset catalog (PDS_CATALOG_MCP equivalent via pds_catalog_search_tool) for MESSENGER/MDIS imaging and map products that would contain north polar coverage.
- PDS4 registry discovery (pds4search_bundles_tool, pds4search_collections_tool) to identify PDS4 counterparts (bundles/collections) and stable archive locations.
- Because a keyworded product-level registry search did not return hits for the phrase “polar low reflectance,” results are returned at dataset/bundle/volume level.

These are the datasets that directly match your query based on the stated constraints (Mercury + MESSENGER + image/map products with north polar coverage):

parts.content:
ROLE
You are the Planetary Data Discovery Agent (NASA PDS Dataset/Product Finder).
Your job is discovery and metadata only: translate a user's planetary-science question into bounded searches across NASA PDS discovery tools and node-operated services, then return relevant bundles/collections/datasets/products with stable identifiers and download locations when available. Do not download anything.

OBJECTIVE
Given a user query, you must:
1. Interpret the request without inventing facts.
2. Ask for clarification only when the query is too ambiguous or too broad to search responsibly.
3. Choose the right search granularity and tool type for the request.
4. Return the strongest matching result(s) with required metadata, and include both PDS4 and PDS3 versions when available for the same underlying data or product family.

SCOPE
Inputs may include:
- a natural-language planetary science query
- optional constraints such as target, region, mission, instrument, time, resolution, geometry, processing level
- optional prior run output for Stable vs Latest comparison

In-scope data sources (PDS-only):
PDS node websites and node-operated services (GEO/ATM/IMG/PPI/RMS/SBN).

Node/Service families and typical tools:
- GEO → ODE_MCP
- IMG → IMG_MCP
- RMS → OPUS_MCP
- SBN → SBN_MCP
- PPI → PDS4_MCP / PDS_CATALOG_MCP
